Double Major and Minor

Double Major Program

MENU

Regulation on the Principles Regarding Transfers
Between Associate and Undergraduate Degree Programs, Double Major, Minor, and
Inter-Institutional Credit Transfer in Higher Education Institutions

(1) (Amended: OG-9/6/2017-30091): “A double major
program may be established between associate degree programs conducted within
the same higher education institution and other associate degree programs,
between undergraduate programs and other undergraduate programs, or between
undergraduate and associate degree programs, upon the recommendation of the
relevant departments and faculty/school boards and with the approval of the
senate.”

(6) (Added: OG-16/8/2011-28027): “The courses and
credits that the student must take in the double major program are determined
with the approval of the senate upon the recommendation of the relevant
departments and faculty boards, taking into account the Higher Education
Qualifications Framework determined by the Council of Higher Education. The
relevant double major undergraduate program must be arranged in a way that
ensures the student possesses the defined learning outcomes based on the
minimum knowledge, skills, and competencies required to be attained at the end
of the program.”

In accordance with this, students who are
enrolled in any undergraduate degree program of the Faculty of Applied Sciences
and meet the admission requirements may pursue education in a second major
program after the evaluation of the Faculty Administrative Board in the
undergraduate program they apply to.

Click to access our university's Double Major Program directive.
